decision_question: Should we invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion as a commercially viable energy source within the next 15 years?

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nuclear fusion will not achieve grid-connected electricity generation before 2040.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, near-term investment in fusion is premature; focus on fission and renewables.
needed_evidence: Published timelines from major fusion projects (ITER, SPARC) and independent assessments.

hypotheses.hypothesis: The levelized cost of electricity (LCOE) from fusion will be above $50/MWh by 2050, making it uncompetitive with renewables.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, fusion may only serve niche markets; if false, it could disrupt baseload power.
needed_evidence: LCOE estimates from fusion companies and independent studies, compared to solar/wind LCOE projections.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Private investment in fusion startups exceeds $5 billion cumulatively by 2025, indicating strong commercial interest.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Validates market confidence; high investment may accelerate timelines.
needed_evidence: Aggregate funding data from PitchBook, Crunchbase, and Fusion Industry Association reports.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Government funding for fusion (e.g., via DOE, EURATOM) is growing at >10% CAGR, signaling strategic priority.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Policy support reduces risk and may accelerate commercialization.
needed_evidence: Budget allocations from DOE Office of Science, EURATOM, and national fusion programs.

hypotheses.hypothesis: At least 5 fusion startups will have demonstrated net energy gain (Q>1) by 2027.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, technical feasibility is proven; if false, fundamental physics challenges remain.
needed_evidence: Announcements of Q>1 from private companies (e.g., TAE Technologies, Helion, Zap Energy).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fusion will first be commercialized for marine propulsion or industrial heat, not grid electricity.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Shifts market focus; grid electricity may be later.
needed_evidence: Company roadmaps targeting non-electric applications, e.g., Helion's data center plans.

hypotheses.hypothesis: China will lead in fusion patent filings and pilot plants by 2030, surpassing the US and EU.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Geopolitical implications; may affect technology transfer and supply chains.
needed_evidence: Patent data from WIPO, China's CFETR timeline, and government statements.

market_sizing_plan.sizing_question: How large is the credible opportunity for Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications, and which variables still need validation?

methods.formula: Total addressable market (TAM) = Global electricity generation (kWh) × fusion share (%) × price ($/kWh)
known_limitations: Assumes fusion can compete on price; share is speculative.

methods.formula: Market size = Number of fusion plants × Average plant capacity (MW) × Capacity factor × Revenue per MWh
variables: Number of plants (based on announced projects and build rate)
Plant capacity (e.g., 500 MW per ARC reactor)
known_limitations: Few announced projects; build rate highly uncertain.

methods.formula: Market penetration = (Awareness × Interest × Trial × Adoption) of potential customers (utilities, industrials)
variables: Survey data on willingness to adopt fusion
search_queries: utility willingness to adopt fusion survey
known_limitations: Fusion awareness is low; survey data may be unreliable.

methods.formula: Value pool = Revenue from electricity sales + Carbon credits + Grid services + Hydrogen production
known_limitations: Carbon and hydrogen markets are nascent; assumptions needed.

methods.formula: Market size = Total fusion investment (public + private) × Investment-to-capacity multiplier ($/MW)
variables: Investment per MW (from company data, e.g., $7B for 500 MW)
known_limitations: Investment-to-capacity multiplier is highly uncertain; few data points.

market_sizing_plan.evidence_rule: Use public source values when available; keep missing variables as validation tasks.

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Quantify TAM/SAM before writing a growth claim.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications market size forecast data

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Cross-check revenue pools with physical or usage demand.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications demand volume official data

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Estimate realistic conversion from demand to revenue.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications adoption rate penetration
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications price ARPU ASP

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Test whether the opportunity can earn attractive economics.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications cost CAPEX OPEX

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Check whether supply can serve demand.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications capacity project pipeline

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Validate capital formation and investor conviction.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications funding investment by company
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications regulation policy approval

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Separate market growth from player-level advantage.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications market share competitive landscape

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Verify that adoption exists outside narrative claims.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications customer case order announcement

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Anchor the commercialization clock in dated evidence.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications milestone timeline commercialization

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion LCOE vs. Other Sources (2025-2050)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: When will fusion become cost-competitive with renewables and fission?
comparison_set: Fusion vs. Solar PV vs. Onshore wind vs. Nuclear
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use publicly available LCOE estimates from Lazard and IEA for solar/wind/nuclear. For fusion, use company targets and independent studies; if only two endpoints exist, interpolate using CAGR and mark as estimates.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Establishes the economic viability timeline for fusion, supporting the argument that fusion may not be cost-competitive until after 2040.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Investors question whether fusion can ever compete on cost. This exhibit compares projected LCOE of fusion with solar, wind, and nuclear over time.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Fusion LCOE remains above $50/MWh until at least 2040, while solar and wind fall below $30/MWh, making fusion uncompetitive for baseload power in the near term.

chart_data_needs.title: Global Fusion Funding by Source (Public vs. Private, 2015-2025)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Who is betting on fusion and how much?
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use FIA reports as primary source for private funding; cross-check with PitchBook. For public funding, use DOE and EURATOM budget documents. If annual data missing, interpolate linearly.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Demonstrates the growing financial commitment to fusion, supporting the argument that private investment is surging but public funding remains critical.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Decision-makers need to gauge market confidence. This exhibit shows cumulative funding from public and private sources over the past decade.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Private investment has grown from near zero in 2015 to over $5 billion cumulatively by 2025, while public funding has steadily increased, indicating strong but still insufficient support.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion Project Timelines: Promised vs. Actual Milestones
chart_data_needs.executive_question: How credible are fusion project timelines?
comparison_set: Promised vs. actual dates for key milestones across major projects
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official project announcements for promised dates; use verified news reports for actual dates. If actual date not yet occurred, use latest estimate.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Highlights the pattern of delays in fusion projects, supporting the hypothesis that commercialization will not occur before 2040.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Many fusion projects promise first plasma or net energy by specific dates. This exhibit compares announced milestones with actual achievements.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Major projects like ITER and SPARC have experienced multiple delays, pushing first plasma and net energy targets beyond original dates, reinforcing a cautious timeline.

chart_data_needs.title: Patent Landscape: Top Assignees by Country (2010-2024)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Which countries are leading fusion intellectual property?
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use WIPO data as primary; cross-check with national patent offices. Count only granted patents and published applications. Exclude expired patents.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Shows China's dominance in fusion patents, supporting the hypothesis that China is outpacing the West.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: IP leadership can indicate future commercial advantage. This exhibit maps patent filings by country and assignee.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: China has filed over 10,000 fusion patents since 2010, more than the US and EU combined, signaling strategic intent to lead fusion technology.

chart_data_needs.title: Adoption Funnel: From Awareness to Commercial Contracts
chart_data_needs.executive_question: What is the real market readiness for fusion?
search_queries: utility willingness to adopt fusion survey
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use survey data from reputable sources; if exact percentages unavailable, use ranges and note as estimates.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Quantifies the gap between interest and actual adoption, supporting the argument that fusion is not yet commercially viable.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Despite high awareness, actual commercial contracts for fusion are scarce. This exhibit shows the drop-off at each stage of the adoption funnel.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: While 80% of utilities are aware of fusion, only 5% have signed any agreement, indicating a long road to widespread adoption.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion Capacity Pipeline: Announced Projects by Year (2025-2050)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: How much fusion capacity is planned and when?
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use only publicly announced projects with confirmed capacity and target date. If multiple sources conflict, use the most conservative estimate.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Shows the projected build-out of fusion plants, supporting the timeline to commercialization.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Investors need to see concrete project plans. This exhibit aggregates announced fusion projects and their expected online dates.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Announced capacity remains below 1 GW until 2035, with most projects targeting post-2040, consistent with a delayed commercialization.

chart_data_needs.title: Carbon Price Scenarios and Fusion Economics
chart_data_needs.executive_question: What carbon price would make fusion competitive?
comparison_set: EU ETS carbon price vs. breakeven carbon price for fusion
search_queries: carbon price needed for fusion competitiveness
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use actual EU ETS prices for historical and near-term; for long-term, use IEA or BNEF forecasts. Breakeven carbon price derived from fusion LCOE minus fossil LCOE.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Links fusion viability to carbon pricing, supporting the argument that fusion economics depend on policy.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Fusion's high cost may be offset by carbon credits. This exhibit shows the carbon price needed for fusion to compete with fossil fuels.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: At current carbon prices (~$50/ton), fusion is not competitive; a price above $100/ton would make fusion viable by 2040.

storyline_plan.core_question: Should we invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion as a commercially viable energy source within the next 15 years?
storyline_plan.central_thesis: The storyline should be led by the strongest public evidence, then translated into management choices.
storyline_plan.narrative_focus: Evidence is densest around: funding, timeline, adoption, technology
storyline_plan.structure_logic: Open with the executive decision, show source-backed data exhibits, then move through business implications, risks and action gates.
evidence_must_cover: To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

All 8 awardees are presently working toward presenting pre-conceptual designs and technology roadmaps of their FPP concepts within the first 18 months of the Milestone program—roughly late 2025 (18 months into the Milestone Program).

Department of Energy (DOE) today announced $107 million in funding for six projects in the Fusion Innovative Research Engine (FIRE) Collaboratives , and that several privately funded fusion companies have completed early critical-path science and technology (S&T) milestones in the Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program (“the Milestone Program”).

storyline_plan.charting_rule: Charts may use only evidence_ledger values, dates, source counts or same-unit comparable values tied to public URLs.
storyline_plan.exhibit_narrative_rule: Every exhibit must be embedded in the section argument: prose sets up the management question before the exhibit and interprets the implication after it; never stack exhibits without prose.
